Here is an example showing the corresponding section in the Manage Governance Processes Business Partners app:

 

 

There are 2 ways to create such a link – both ensuring the high quality of that information:

  • As a user using the Look Up app to choose the right Reference Record and then creating a link. Manually typing in such a number in the BP is not possible.
  • In the background, during the Reference Data Lookup step the link is created by the system.

The Reference Data Lookup step trusts this link over other business data. If the business partner contains a reference record link, the data provider should return the current data for this linked reference record and not search to find a better match to another reference record.

In the example above (from the very beginning), the "4 ID numbers added across 2 BPs" contain 2 business numbers (one being Commercial Register Number - BUP002, HRB 20552) and 2 Reference Record Links (one being Corporate Data Quality - DPI001, GR.DE.RC:B8536_HRB722007).

 

DEMO

See below a video for different ways to work with the results of the Reference Data Lookup step. Click the image or follow the link Reference Data Lookup step in action.

 

Other related information

  • Blog Benefit From SAP Master Data Governance on SAP S/4HANA 2023 Feature Pack Stack 02 talking about MDG wide latest innovations in a nutshell
  • Blog Efficient Mass-Enrichment of Business Partners into SAP MDG: A Comprehensive Guide from our development partner CDQ

Author

Martin Steinhauser, Product Owner for Master Data Quality Services focussing on Data Provider Integration topics.

entry.title: Discover TechEd “Live” at SAPinsider EMEA 2024
entry.content:
Following hot on the heels of the hugely successful SAP TechEd Virtual event last month, attention now turns to a number of live events that will feature TechEd content and speakers, starting at the ASUG Tech Connect event in West Palm Beach, the first of SAP’s “TechEd on Tour” locations, as well as featuring in sessions at the SAPinsider EMEA “TechInsider” event in Copenhagen. Both events run from 12- 14 November in these wonderful, if quite different, locations.

I’ve been deeply involved in the planning for the SAPinsider event again, and for this reason alone will focus my blog on that event, though I am hopeful that both events will deliver a rich experience of TechEd content for attendees, and allow them to delve deeply into SAP technologies, particularly relating to SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) and Artificial Intelligence (AI).

At SAPinsider Copenhagen we’re going the extra mile, in trying to deliver a TechEd-style experience for attendees, who I’m happy to say will not only find an agenda littered with TechEd sessions, delivered by many of our stellar TechEd speakers, but also they’ll be able to participate in multiple hands-on sessions as well as having an opportunity to continue their learning experience by networking with SAP’s Developer Advocates who will guide and support attendees as they work through a number of the tutorials available at the Developer Garage on the show floor – which we are pleased will make its very first appearance at this SAPinsider event.

With all this on offer I’m excited for what’s in store in Copenhagen next week – and I hope that you are too!

Plan Ahead – And Set Your Agenda Now!

For those of you registered for the event, and in a manner which is not unlike visiting a Disney theme park, my suggestion for you to get the most out of an SAPinsider event is to plan ahead – not so much to beat the queues as you might at the theme park, but more so to make sure that you see all you want to see, and achieve what you set out to do. And the best way to do this is to prepare your agenda for the event before you go.

The first step is of course to get the event app, “SAPinsider Events”, available on the App Store and Google Play, and once you’ve installed that you’re good to go in terms of adding sessions to your personal agenda. Now for the fun bit – what sessions to see? Perhaps I might help with some ideas.

I’ll assume that everyone will plan to join the day 1 and day 2 keynotes. I’m looking forward to both, the motivational first day keynote from Hein Wagner, starting at 9.00am on Tuesday 12th November, followed the next day by the AI inspired second day keynote, featuring Martin Böhm of Ottobock and Jonathan Von Rüden of SAP, and which starts at the same time of day on the 13th November.

But after the keynotes, what next? Here are my personal 6 TechEd inspired picks for Copenhagen:

  1. Enhancing SAP Development Processes with SAP Build and ABAP Cloud Interoperability to Elevate Your Extensions, 12-Nov-24, 10:30 - 11:15
  2. Hands-On Lab: Develop Applications and Processes with SAP Build and Joule, 12-Nov-24, 15:30 - 17:30
  3. Mastering SAP Business AI for Enhanced Performance and Innovation, 13-Nov-24, 10:30 - 11:15
  4. SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Datasphere Solutions: Integration, 13-Nov-24, 14:15 - 15:00
  5. Clean Core Extensibility for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, 13-Nov-24, 16:45 - 17:30
  6. SAP BTP Strategy and Highlights, 14-Nov-24, 09:00 - 09:45

Of course, this is just the tip of the iceberg, as there are many more sessions available for you to select from the agenda, and among which you’ll find more TechEd content, as well as content from other speakers from SAP and our business partners, in addition to many customer case stories too. And in a shameless plug too, you may also note my session “Navigating Your Path to Success with SAP BTP” which I shall deliver alongside Nis Boy Naeve on 13 November at 11:30am. Please join us if you can! Take a look at the SAPinsider event website sessions list, or the event app that you’ve already downloaded to see more.

Your Continued Learning Experience

If you’d like to continue your own personal learning journey after the event, then why not explore other learning opportunities from SAP? For example, learn how to build software applications, side-by-side extensions, and integrations to and from cloud applications by exploring SAP’s free learning content on SAP BTP. It is made for both integration designers and extension developers from all levels of expertise and will help you stay up to date with the latest SAP BTP innovations. And check out even more role-based learning resources and opportunities to get certified in one place on SAP Learning site.

Or if you’re interested in TechEd sessions, but can’t join the events in West Palm Beach or Copenhagen, then perhaps another TechEd on Tour location, in Australia, Germany or the UK would work better for you?

For those of you travelling to Copenhagen next week, I do hope that you find SAPinsider EMEA 2024 to be a truly informative and stimulating experience. And, of course, if you visit the show floor then look me up at the SAP exhibit booth, and I’ll be happy to have a chat with you about SAP BTP, the conference or just about the amazing city of Copenhagen!

My best wishes for safe travels, and I hope that you enjoy the event!

Chris Grundy

Product Marketing, SAP BTP

entry.title: SAP Master Data Governance: A Leader Among Users
entry.content:
SAP Master Data Governance (MDG) has consistently been rated as a Leader in the Fall 2024 G2 Grid for Master Data Management (MDM) on the G2 peer to peer review site, and the reasons are clear.

First and foremost, SAP MDG offers a comprehensive and intuitive platform for managing multi-domain master data across the enterprise. With its user-friendly interface and robust capabilities, organizations can easily create, update, and maintain their business-critical master data in a centralized and controlled environment, while leveraging 3rd data quality and enrichment services to automate the process.

One of the key features that sets SAP MDG apart is its ability to streamline data governance processes and ensure data quality and consistency. The platform offers a wide range of data governance capabilities, including data validation, standardization, correction, enrichment, and consolidation, which helps organizations maintain high-quality and accurate master data thru a single lens.  The solution also provides data quality management, enabling organizations to define master data quality rules, striving for consistent and validated data across all points of entry while also enabling data quality monitoring and remediation.

Another key feature of SAP MDG is its tight integration with other SAP solutions, which allows organizations to leverage existing data models, business logic, and validation frameworks. This integration not only streamlines data management processes but also enhances collaboration between different departments by providing a unified view of master data. Furthermore, the application supports open integration with third-party products and services, ensuring that organizations can maintain a flexible and adaptable technology stack. Its combination of centralized governance with decentralized ownership through federation, along with seamless integration capabilities, positions it as a valuable asset for any enterprise aiming to optimize its data landscape and drive better business outcomes.

Lastly, the robust support and training resources offered by SAP further contribute to the platform's high user satisfaction ratings. Users appreciate the strong customer support and extensive training materials that are available to help them maximize the value of SAP MDG within their organizations.

Overall, SAP Master Data Governance's ability to centralize and streamline master data management processes, its seamless integration with other SAP applications, and its strong support and training resources all contribute to its high user satisfaction. As more organizations recognize the importance of effective master data management, it's no surprise that SAP MDG continues to be a leader in the master data management market space among users.

Learn more:

  • SAP Master Data Governance on G2
  • SAP Community for SAP Master Data Governance

entry.title: What's New in SAP Master Data Governance, cloud edition 2411?
entry.content:
SAP Master Data Governance, cloud edition is a software-as-a-service (Saas) solution on SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) that is used to govern core attributes of business partner data (more domains planned). It is perfectly suited for organizations kick-starting a master data management initiative at low entry barriers. For details, see also the SAP Master Data Governance, cloud edition overview and  deep-dive presentations.

What's New?

Continuing the blog series about innovations in SAP Master Data Governance, cloud edition, I'd like to provide you with details on the latest innovations with SAP Master Data Governance, cloud edition 2411. 

General Remark

Generally, a new version of several existing apps is available to provide new functionalities, for example, simulation of validation rules and additional process attributes. You can recognize the previous versions that have now been deprecated by the Deprecated label on the tiles. Note that a user can only use either all deprecated or all new versions when working with SAP Master Data Governance, cloud edition. We recommend using the new version of all apps as soon as possible, as the deprecated versions will be removed in the next release.

Updates in Data Quality Management

Data quality management comes with new apps to support derivation scenarios, validation rules and analytics, and a new version of the validation rules app. 

New and enhanced apps in data quality management

In detail, the increased scope in data quality management for business partner data, enables master data specialists to:

  • Define derivation scenarios
    • With the new Define Derivation Scenarios for Business Partners app, you can create and edit derivation scenarios for your business partner data. You use derivation scenarios to deduce master data based on rules and a defined scope to ensure data quality.
  • Import/export of derivation scenarios
    • With the new Import Derivation Scenarios app, you can import derivation scenarios and their rules, which you previously downloaded using the Export Validation Rules app. Validation rules, their business details and their BRFplus implementation can be imported.

Importing derivation scenarios - log details

  • Import/export of validation rules
    • With the new Import Validation Rules app, you can import validation rules, which you previously downloaded using the Export Validation Rules app. Validation rules, their business details and their BRFplus implementation can be imported.
    • With the new Export Validation Rules app, you can download validation rules, which are managed in the Validation Rules for Business Partners app, to then import them into another system. Validation rule exports contain validation rules, their business details and their BRFplus implementation.

Exporting validation rules

  • The new version of the Validation Rules for Business Partners app offers the following new features:
    • Copy validation rules
    • Simulate validation rules to check if they work as intended and to get a preview of the rule results
    • Check if the rule is assigned to any dimensions, which is done in the Configure Data Quality Scores for Business Partners app descibed in the following:
    • The new Configure Data Quality Scores app allows key users to define their data quality reporting structure by using Categories and Dimensions. The aggregation of rules scores to data quality dimensions and categories is as follows:
Categories
  • Multiple categories supported
  • Score: Average of dimension scores 
  • Thresholds: Target, Warning, Critical 
Dimensions
  • Multiple dimensions supported 
  • Score: weighted average of rule scores 
Rules
  • Assigned to dimension with Impact
  • Multiple assignments possible, but only once per category

 Improvements in Business Partner Hierarchy Management

New features in Track Changes – Business Partner Hierarchies and Track Changes – Business Partner Hierarchy Nodes apps

  • A value help was added to the Business Partner Hierarchy and Business Partner Hierarchy Node fields. In the Track Changes - Business Partner Hierarchy Nodes app, you can search for a hierarchy ID or hierarchy description to find the associated nodes.

New features in new Manage Business Partner Hierarchies app

  • The following new features are available in the new version of the Manage Business Partner Hierarchies app:

    • The node ID is now generated automatically if you leave the field empty. The generated node ID is based on the hierarchy ID and a counter.

    • The system can generate node descriptions automatically. To generate one, add a business partner to a node and choose Accept. The first and second word of the business partner will be used as node description.
    • The search filters Business Partner and Hierarchy Node ID are now available. These filters can help you to find the associated hierarchy.
    • You can now edit a hierarchy that is in a process in the Process Information tab. In this tab, you can change the Process Description, Process Template, Priority, Due Date, and Reason.
    • When you make changes to your hierarchy, you can now see the Node Status column in the app. Edited nodes are displayed as Changed or Inserted. Deleted nodes disappear from the hierarchy.

Additional Improvements in Business Partner Processing

Additional innovations include:

  • With the new My Governance Processes - Business Partner app you can display business partner processes that have been started by you. The system displays the list of processes as well as the number of business partners, BP relationships and hierarchies.

My Governance Processes app showing all running processes initiated by oneself

  • With a new feature in the Maintain process attributes for Manage Business Partners and Manage Business Processes apps you can now maintain special attributes such as priority, due date or reason to categorize and analyze master data processes when you create or change a business partner. This information can be changed as long as the process is not activated or cancelled.

Categorization of master data creation process by priority, reason, and due date

For an overview of the innovations specified above, see also the What's New Viewer - SAP Master Data Governance, Cloud Edition 2411

Continued innovations in SAP Master Data Governance, cloud edition help organizations in managing their core business partner data master for their entire system landscape. 

To stay on track, simply follow the SAP Community Topic Page for SAP Master Data Governance, read the MDG-related blogs, and join the  Q&A forum.

Best,

Markus

entry.title: Benefits of SAP MDG Solution
entry.content:
Benefits of SAP MDG Solution

Here are the key benefits of SAP's Master Data Governance (MDG) solution

  • SAP MDG performs effective master data management. It helps to improve data quality, consistency, a

     

    nd accuracy. Many industries use SAP MDG to increase customer experience, reduce costs, enhance productivity, and speed up new product launches.
  • Flexibility is one of the key benefits of SAP MDG. SAP MDG allows customization. It is a centralized platform that helps manage master data across various domains such as HRM, ERP, CRM, and supply chain.
  • SAP MDG supports managing both structured and unstructured data. So you can manage customer and product data, emails, and so on.

Central Governance

You can create and maintain master data with SAP MDG. You can streamline master data using a central system that adheres to data rules and standards. With this approach, master data is maintained in a central system and then replicated in satellite systems. 

It ensures that the downstream system transactions leverage master data, as shown in the below image.

Know that SAP MDG manages the master data through a clear audit trail. It provides significant business benefits, improving business operations.

SAP MDG helps to avoid error-prone manual maintenance on master data. It offers centralized governance and data replication to systems. Moreover, it ensures data consistency and maps master data across entire enterprise systems.

 

Consolidation for Analytics Purpose Only

Master data is usually created in standalone or transactional systems based on needs. You must consolidate and centralize the master data for data analytics. It can be done by merging and mapping the data to standard data. The image below explains the same.

It enables organizations to easily manage and maintain a single version of the truth for all their critical data. 

This approach allows organizations to gain valuable insights and make informed decisions with accurate and reliable data.

 

 

Consolidation for Initial Load Before Central Governance

It is necessary to merge and create the best record to consolidate master data in the central governance scenario. You can achieve the same by using the consolidation functionalities of SAP MDG. It is usually a one-time activity in which companies manage their master data and replicate it into multiple systems.

The consolidation scenario helps to extract, cleanse, and consolidate data during the initial load. It helps prepare the existing data for central governance, ensuring it is accurate reliable, and adheres to data rules and standards. 

With SAP MDG, organizations can streamline this process, enabling them to easily manage and maintain their master data and replicate it across multiple systems.

 

Consolidation for Mergers or Acquisitions

Introducing new systems into an enterprise's landscape can create data harmonization and duplication challenges.

However, companies can synchronize and de-duplicate new data with the existing data using the consolidation functionalities of SAP MDG. Using SAP MDG, enterprises can merge and map data to a common data standard, ensuring that new data is consistent and accurate with the existing data. This helps avoid the creation of duplicate records, ensuring that the data remains clean and reliable. 

By using SAP MDG's consolidation functionalities, organizations can streamline harmonizing and de-duplicating new data. It enables organizations to quickly integrate new systems and leverage the full potential of their master data.

Continuous Hybrid Approach

If you want to refine master data from source systems, you must combine the consolidation and central governance scenarios. It helps to manage the enhanced data using a significant governance process. 

SAP Master Data Governance offers several functionalities to support various use cases, including:

  • Business-driven workflow and workflow rules framework to automate master data management
  • Reducing implementation costs by reusing data validation as well as mapping logic.
  • Integrate with SAP data model for key master data domains
  • Customize master data objects' configuration frameworks to optimize data management.
  • Provide extension capabilities for standard master data objects
  • Provide APIs and integration capabilities to access data and use change logs.
  • monitoring data management processes for built-in master data stewardship tools
  • managing data quality through business rules repository
  • analyzing data quality for product and master records
  • correcting data quality issues through worklists and embedded analytics

 

 

Differences Between SAP MDG and SAP MDM

Here are the key differences between MDM and MDG:   

Criteria

MDM

MDG

Definition

It ensures the quality, consistency, and accuracy of data across the enterprise

It provides a way to manage master data in multiple systems while maintaining consistency

Data Storage

You can store data in one central location

You can store data in multiple systems

Data Distribution

Data is distributed from the central location to other systems

Data is replicated across multiple systems

Single Version of Truth

Provides a single version of truth for data

Provides consistency for data stored in multiple systems

Use Case

Useful when you need to synchronize data across the organization

Useful when you need to maintain data consistency across multiple systems

 

 

                                                                                                                                            Published By

                                                                                                                                            Nikhat Fatima

entry.title: Automate your core Master Data creation processes with SAP MDG cloud edition and Derivations
entry.content:
Hi,

Some great news for the SAP BTP SAAS service “SAP Master Data Governance, cloud edition“! As officially announced on this page What's New Viewer - SAP Master Data Governance, Cloud Edition:

SAP Master Data Governance, cloud edition now supports DQM Derivation Scenarios.

Many MDG experts already know this capability from the SAP MDG Private cloud edition and know the strength of this feature.

 

Introduction

SAP has now released the following Fiori Apps in SAP Master Data Governance, Cloud Edition in that space:

  1. Define Derivation Scenarios for Business Partners
  2. Import Derivation Scenarios and Export Derivation Scenarios

With the app “Define Derivation Scenarios for Business Partners” you can now implement (no code) business rules to derive business partner data automatically in a background step of a MDG process. The following SAP standard process templates include the “derive step”:

  • SAP_BP_COA SAP: Consolidate BP Automated
  • SAP_BP_MA SAP: Mass Processing BP Automated
  • SAP_BX_COA SAP: Consolidate BP+Rel. Automated
  • SAP_BX_SA SAP: Single Processing BP Automated
  • SAP_BX_DCA SAP: Decentr.Create BP+Rel.w/Mtchg Autom
  • SAP_BX_DUA SAP: Decentr. Update BP+Rel. Autom.

Before: Process without derivations

Business example without derivations:

 

Step 1: Before the automation was defined the business end users used MDG cloud edition to create new Business Partner manually.

Step 2: Automation - In the past the end users had the chance to derived BP data like Name, Tax Number and Address data from an external repository like CDQ. That automation already increased the process performance and optimized the data quality

Step 3-6: Manual activities - After that step the end users had to manually create some more BP data like BP-Roles, assignment to Industry sectors and company codes and organizational data (Sales Area for the customer part and purchasing data for suppliers)

Step 7: Finally the end user can submit the request.

Summary: The whole process took a reasonable amount of time and was pretty error-prone because sometimes the end users forgot to assign some entities.

After: Process with MDG Derivations:

Same result as above but with derivations:

 

Step 1: Business end users uses MDG cloud edition to trigger a new creation process for a Business Partner.

Step 2: Automation - As before: MDG cloud edition is doing a lookup of BP data like Name, Tax Number and Address data from an external repository.

Step 3: Submit request – End user doesn’t have to enter more master data attributes because they will be derived in the next step

Step 4: Automation – The MDG Process will execute the derivation scenario. By this the business rules are applied within seconds and no user input required.

Summary: The new process with derivation will decrease the time to create new master data and in addition in a higher data quality.

Scenario Details

Screenshots from the design time app “Define Derivation Scenarios for Business Partners”

1. MDG Derivation Scenario level

Comments:

  • In Derivation Rules for Fields: Derive single values for attributes on level BUT000
  • Derivation Rules for Tables: The scenario will derive (create new table lines) for various tables like BUT100, BUT0IS, MDC_KNVV, MDC_KNB1, MDC_LFB1 and MDC_LFM1
  • For each of the tables a rule is created.
  • Each of the rule can be managed/governed individually by an status based process.

2. Details for the derivation rule “Derive Industries for Organizations”

 

Comments:

  • Condition Fields (eg BUT000-TYPE) can be used to define additional conditions on rule level. In this example you can define different rules for different conditions. An example is that an BP of type Person gets different Industry Sectors (or no?) assigned compared to organizations.
  • Result Fields are the attributes which are derived by the system according to the decision table (see next screenshot)

3. Details for the BRF+ Decision table for Industries

Here you can review how to define the business rule without coding in BRF+

 

If the BP is of type Organization, the system will derive 2 new Industries for it. No ABAP coding with MDG API required. In a SAAS solution BADI coding option is not available anyways.

4. Details for the BRF+ Decision table for Company Codes

Comment:

The column 1 "Condition Decision Table for TABLE" is maintained in the "Condition table" to have more flexibility. In this example "DUMMY"  means that for all BPs (executed in the process and within the scenario condition expression) the two Company Codes entries will be derived. Since newer version of DQM Derivation Scenarios this DUMMY is not need anymore. 

Screenshots of the new process with derivations

1. Start the creation process

 

Comments:

  • With this app you can create a new BP from scratch, or you use the “Look Up and Create” feature.
  • You can also search for existing BPs and send them into a change process.

 

2. Retrieve the lookup data from external data provider

 

Comments:

  • See help: https://help.sap.com/docs/mdg-ce/administration-guide/integrating-reference-data-for-business-partners
  • You do a search on the data provider within the MDG UI and select the correct BP in the list. By using the “Create Business Partner” button the data will get copied to your request form. It will take over all the data from the provider like address, organizational names, Tax and ID Number.

3. Submit the request

 

Comments:

  • The end user can leave the entities for Industries, BP-Roles and organizational data empty because the system will derive the data after the submit.

 

4. Review the automatic derivation

This is the UI Inbox task as presented to the approver:

 

Comments:

  • The derivations have been successfully executed and the system defaulted the values for Organizational information. Also the BP-Roles are derived.

This is a screenshot of the derivation step in the “Manage Governance Processes” App:

 

Comments:

  • You can see that the derivation affected 7 tables.
  • The green color identifies that new rows have been added. For BP-Roles you can see that 4 new rows (BP-Roles) have been added.
  • In this app you can do further drills downs to see all the details for the derivations.

 

5. Check the record created by process after automation.

The following huge screenshot highlight in red all the data which have been derived by derivation and in green the derived values from the data provider look up. In this example process the system was able to derive >95% of the data without any end user input.

 

Next steps for you

If you are interested in this feature I recommend checking the following documentation:

  • What's New Viewer - SAP Master Data Governance, Cloud Edition - Help
  • SAP Master Data Governance - Community
  • SAP MDG How To Guides
  • Blog from @Sabine_Stellma https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/automate-your-master-data-process-with-derivation-scenarios-in-sap-master/ba-p/13538990

With the updates on Q4/2024 for the SAP Master Data Governance, cloud edition the SAAS solution got some more interesting features. Please read this What's New Viewer - SAP Master Data Governance, Cloud Edition

You can also test MDG cloud edition as a FREE TIER: Follow this guide to get you hands onto MDG cloud edition.

Cheers
Steffen

++ Many thanks for reviewing the content of this blog to @Sabine_Stellma and @Mark63  ++

Video of the end user process: https://video.sap.com/media/t/1_yhdkgjr2

entry.title: Update on Reference Solution Architecture for SAP Master Data Governance
entry.content:
Update on Reference Solution Architecture for SAP Master Data Governance 

We are excited to announce the release of the new version of the Reference Solution Architecture for Master Data Governance (MDG), which has recently been published on Business Accelerator Hub and on the SAP Signavio Process Navigator - SAP for Me.

This updated version includes new solution variants for SAP Master Data Governance on SAP S/4HANA in classic mode and in cloud-ready mode. One of the key highlights of this release is the inclusion of detailed diagrams for business partner, product, and financial domain for master data governance in classic mode. These diagrams provide a comprehensive overview of the solution architecture, making it easier for users to understand how MDG fits into their existing IT landscape and how it can be integrated with other systems and applications.  

Updates in Detail

1. Solution Variant “Federated Master Data Governance with Core Data Owner on SAP S/4HANA”

We added the information that Federated Master Data Governance can also be used with MDG on lower releases/classic-mode. More information can be found in the how-to guide and in the blog "Consumption of Federated Governance Made Easy" from @Mark63.

In the next two solution variants, we have replaced the existing one for “Centrally Managed Master Data Governance on SAP S/4HANA” with two new solution variants to illustrate the supported processes: 

2. Solution Variant “Centrally Managed Master Data Governance on SAP S/4HANA – Classic Mode”

In the Solution Value Flow Diagram, you can see that MDG on SAP S/4HANA, classic mode supports multiple domains, including business partner, product, and financials. This diagram provides a clear representation of how a business process variant is implemented and supported by a specific solution. 

As example you can see the modelled Solution Process Flow Diagrams for Decentral Creation and Decentral Change of Business Partners. In the case of Decentral Creation, a consolidation process is used, whereas for Decentral Change, the MDG, Central Governance Change Request is used. 

The descriptive text next to the diagram contains more information: It provides details about the standard delivered process template and change request types that are suitable for decentralized creation and change processes.  

3. Solution Variant “Centrally Managed Master Data Governance on SAP S/4HANA – Cloud-Ready Mode”

In comparison to the Solution Variant for MDG on SAP S/4HANA, classic mode you can see the Solution Value Flow Diagram for cloud-ready mode currently only contains the Business Partner Domain. 

The Solution Process Flow Diagrams were initially published last year. We have now included three new diagrams, which demonstrate the support for Mass Load, Mass Change, and Mass Deletion of Business Partner Relationships. 

Furthermore, to draw a comparison with classic mode diagrams, here you can see the modelled Solution Process Flow Diagrams for Decentral Creation and Decentral Change of Business Partners in cloud-ready mode. In both cases, consolidation processes are used. 

We believe that this latest release will be a valuable resource for organizations looking to implement or expand their MDM capabilities. By providing a clear and comprehensive overview of the solution architecture, we aim to empower users to make the most of their MDM investment and to achieve their data management goals more efficiently.  

We invite you to explore the new Reference Solution Architecture for MDM by yourself on the Business Accelerator Hub and on the SAP Signavio Process Navigator - SAP for Me.

Further information

Curious about the initially released content and about the different diagram types such as Solution Value Flow diagram, Solution Component diagram, Solution Data Flow diagram, Solution Process Flow diagram? 

  • Check the blog post “Explore Reference Solution Architecture for Master Data Governance” from my colleague @KarinBlank. 
  • Watch our Video: How to use Reference Solution Architecture for SAP Master Data Governance - YouTube 

To stay up to date, simply follow the SAP Community Topic Page for SAP Master Data Governance, join the Questions & Answers forum and Blog space, and check the MDG roadmap for all MDG versions. 

Best, Christina Lindner

entry.title: Efficient Mass-Enrichment of Business Partners into SAP MDG: A Comprehensive Guide
entry.content:
What happens when you need to repeat this process 100,000 times for all of your business partners? Do you enter 100,000 addresses, choose the best match 100,000 times, download the data 100,000 times manually, and create 100,000 change requests?

At this scale, an easy process quickly becomes impractical, likely overwhelming your master data department’s resources for months. And by the time they’re finished, the data may have already changed, forcing the process to start all over again.

So, the question is: How can the mass-loading of business partners be made more efficient?

Data enrichment of large volumes of business partner master data into SAP MDG is critical for organizations managing existing business partner bases or integrating new acquisitions. The CDQ-SAP partnership addresses this challenge with a specialized mass-enrichment feature that allows organizations to efficiently load, validate, and enrich data from trusted sources.

This article outlines the process of mass-loading trusted business partner data into SAP MDG, leveraging CDQ’s data integration to ensure high data quality, reduce manual intervention, and maintain up-to-date records.

What Exactly Is Trusted External Data?

In most countries, businesses must register with local authorities to legally operate, trade, employ workers, or market their services. These registers are the primary source of truth for a company's name, tax obligations, operational locations, and legal structure. Any changes must be officially updated in these registers. The types of registers vary by country: some have both legal and tax registers, some have national registers, while others, particularly federations, maintain separate registers for each state. Despite these differences, nearly all registers share a common challenge: they each have their own data standards, interfaces, and access methods.

Integrating data from these diverse registers can be overwhelming. Even for large organizations, managing integrations with each register is often impractical. This is where CDQ comes in. CDQ integrates both official registers and commercial data providers, mapping their various data fields into a unified standard format. This approach allows businesses to access critical data seamlessly, without the need for a massive, resource-intensive integration project. SAP connects to CDQ's service, embedding this streamlined access directly into their software solutions.

Business Partner Master Data: Mass Enrichment into MDG

When uploading large volumes of trusted data into SAP MDG, handling each record manually isn’t sufficient. SAP and CDQ have developed a mass-enrichment process designed to manage mass loads effectively, whether onboarding new data from acquired entities or updating existing records. CDQ connects to trusted data sources while SAP MDG handles the software side, providing an out-of-the-box solution that minimizes the need for extensive customization.

Key Takeaways

  • Scalability: Mass-enriching addresses the limitations of manual single-record updates, making large-scale data loads efficient and manageable.
  • Data Integrity: The process includes quality checks and data enrichment steps to ensure records are up-to-date and accurate.
  • Ease of Use: The solution is designed for minimal technical support, enabling teams to handle mass loads independently.

Process Overview

SAP's Cloud Ready Mode includes robust Master Data Management (MDM) processes that enable centralized, high-quality, and governed data across the organization. MDM ensures accurate, consistent data across systems, improves data quality through validation and deduplication, and supports compliance with audit trails and change tracking. MDM also facilitates data synchronization and integration with business processes, enhancing analytics and decision-making.

In this article, we want to look at the “SAP_BP_COA SAP: Consolidate BP Automated” process, which is designed to enhance the information available about business partner master data in SAP MDG. It serves two main purposes:

  1. Onboarding New Business Partners: When a new business partner is added, this process enriches the initial data, ensuring that all relevant information is included from the start. This can include details like contact information, business addresses, and any other important information related to the partner.
  2. Updating Existing Business Partners: For business partners that are already in the system, this process ensures their data is kept up to date. This is especially useful when information changes, such as a company name, location, or other key details, which helps maintain data accuracy and relevance.

An important aspect of this process is that it doesn’t rely on a universal or global identification system to track these partners. Instead, it uses whatever identification numbers already available for each partner (like tax IDs, company registration numbers, or any unique ID the partner has) to identify and enrich their records. This makes it flexible and adaptable to various data sources without requiring new or complex ID systems.

This process handles the complete load of business partner master data for both Initial Loads and Data Updates, making it ideal for supporting Master Data migration projects, ongoing data updates, and the import of business partner master data in mergers and acquisitions (M&A) projects, where integrating data from new entities is critical.

1. Initial Check

The initial check is crucial for setting a solid foundation for the data upload process. This step begins with uploading business partner records using a standardized Excel template. Often, bulk data imports contain diverse data issues such as duplicate records, incomplete entries, internal comments, and outdated information. The initial check process automatically reviews each record, identifying and flagging any missing or inconsistent data fields.

For example, the initial check verifies if all necessary fields are present and identifies where data is missing. It focuses on the quality and format of the input data to ensure consistency and completeness. By ensuring that critical fields like addresses, tax numbers, and company names are properly filled, the initial check helps lay the groundwork for a more effective mass-enrichment process. Only records with all required information proceed, reducing errors and ensuring accurate data quality from the start.

Key Takeaways

  • Data Quality: Prevents common data issues from affecting later steps in the mass-enrichment process.
  • Efficiency: Cleansed input data ensures that resources are not wasted on unnecessary corrections down the line.

2. Reference Data Lookup

This step performs a detailed look-up against external reference databases to identify essential information like tax numbers, registration identifiers, and other legal markers for each business partner. Here, CDQ connects with a variety of trusted data sources based on your chosen configuration. Depending on the sourcing strategy, data may be retrieved from global providers like D&B or Moody’s, free government registers, or specialized data collectors such as the Legal Entity Identifier (LEI) database.

For instance, a German subsidiary of a multinational corporation might be listed in both local German business registers and in a global database with Moody’s. The Reference Data Lookup can cross-reference identifiers across these sources, ensuring that each entry aligns with official records. This validation is vital for detecting duplicate or erroneous entries that may otherwise go unnoticed.

Key Takeaways

  • Data Accuracy: Cross-checks with authoritative sources improve data accuracy and reliability.
  • Flexible Sourcing: Adaptable to your preferred sources, whether international, regional, or industry-specific.

3. Address Data Validation

During this step, the system verifies and updates address information to ensure alignment with current data from trusted external sources. Often, addresses in a company’s internal records can become outdated as businesses relocate, merge, or undergo other structural changes. The Address Data Validation step matches existing records with the latest official data, allowing users to revalidate and, if necessary, rematch each record.

For example, suppose a company record lists an address that hasn't been updated in years. Through validation, MDG identifies that the company has relocated to a new address or changed its legal structure. The record can then be updated in bulk, ensuring that your address data reflects the most current information. This process can be applied to individual records or to entire data sets, giving users the flexibility to clean up old data systematically.

Key Takeaways

  • Up-to-Date Records: Maintains accurate address data to support contact and regulatory compliance.
  • Improved Data Governance: Reduces data decay by systematically updating outdated entries.

4. Matching and Match Group Filtering

In this step, each business partner record in your input file matched with records from CDQ’s trusted data sources. Here’s how it works:

  1. Initial Data Transfer: The process starts by taking key information from each master data record, including the partner's name, address, and any available identifiers (such as tax IDs or registration numbers). This information is sent to CDQ for processing.
  2. Golden Record Creation: CDQ then applies advanced algorithms to create a "golden record" for each entity. This golden record represents the best and most accurate version of the entity, combining information from multiple sources to ensure completeness and reliability.
  3. Returning the Best Match: After creating the golden record, CDQ identifies the best possible match from its trusted sources. It sends back the best candidate record, enriched with the most up-to-date and relevant data, so you have the highest quality information available for each business partner.

This matching process also includes a Match Group Filtering feature. By grouping records based on match quality, it lets users filter results to focus on the most relevant matches and avoid enriching records of inactive entities. This is particularly helpful because many registers flag businesses that are no longer operational, allowing the system to handle such cases efficiently.

For example, during this step, a company with multiple branches might appear as separate entries in various registers, each associated with a slightly different set of data points. MDG can group these records and allow you to select only the active, relevant records for further enrichment. This customization helps avoid overloading your system with unnecessary data and keeps inactive or irrelevant records out of the active database.

Key Takeaways

  • Data Governance: Ensures only active and relevant entities are kept for further processing.
  • Match Customization: Fine-tunes matching criteria to maintain data relevance and quality.

5. Best Record Calculation, Derivation, and Validation

At this stage, CDQ's "golden record" technology aggregates data from multiple sources to generate a comprehensive record that combines the most accurate and relevant information. By chaining identifiers from trusted sources, CDQ creates an enhanced record that pulls in data from various official registers and commercial providers, if available.

For example, if a company is listed in both the official German Commercial Register and D&B with slight variations in registered office addresses or other details, CDQ’s golden record technology will combine these inputs to produce a unified and complete record. This “best of all worlds” approach creates a robust record that minimizes data gaps, allowing organizations to include additional identifiers such as LEIs to strengthen data consistency across international jurisdictions.

Key Takeaways

  • Data Enrichment: Consolidates multiple sources to create a highly detailed, unified record.
  • Real-Time Updates: Pulls data from active sources, ensuring accuracy without relying on outdated static files.

6. Activation

The final activation step prepares enriched records for deployment by allowing users to perform a final quality assessment. SAP MDG summarizes any missing elements or data inconsistencies for each enriched record, providing an overview of potential issues and allowing for a final review. Users can drill down into individual records to examine data points in detail or refine search parameters and enrichment settings if certain records need adjustments.

For instance, suppose the data from a particular register is missing a crucial identifier for a small subset of records. You may choose to manually investigate or rerun specific steps for just those records to ensure complete accuracy. Once satisfied, the enriched data can be activated, replacing outdated records and integrating seamlessly with SAP MDG’s data governance framework.

Key Takeaways

  • Final Quality Assurance: Ensures the activated data meets established quality benchmarks.
  • Flexible Activation: Offers control over which records to activate, allowing for targeted improvements in data governance.

Conclusion

The SAP MDG mass-enrichment feature, in partnership with CDQ’s data integration capabilities, transforms the efficiency of business partner Business partner master data management. By standardizing the approach, SAP and CDQ put the experience of decades of master data projects into a software- and data foundation that can now be used by MDG user organizations. SAP is laying the foundation for an efficiency-gain in large master data projects. With access to a range of reliable international data sources, this solution empowers organizations to implement tailored data sourcing strategies without vendor lock-in. The combination of data quality, flexibility, and ease of use makes this process a valuable asset for both initial data loads and ongoing data updates. Explore our other articles to learn more about leveraging SAP MDG and CDQ for business partner master data Management.

Related Articles:

  • Mass enrichment of business partners with Tax and ID numbers found using the CDQ data provider
  • Hands-on with Free Tiers: CDQ Trusted Business Partner Data in SAP Master Data Governance
  • Trusted Business Partner Data in SAP Master Data Governance, cloud edition
